As shown in Table \ref{tab:city-acceptability-self-d-high-vs-low-tf-sw}, a number of cells deviated from normality. TODO: TRY TO HEAL IT BY TRANSFORMING


```{r city-tyacceptability-self-d-high-vs-low-tf-aov-calculate}

aov.social.type.token.sequential.acceptability.self.d.high.vs.low.tf <- aov(
    d ~  myValence, 
    data = dat.social.type.token.sequential.m.d %>% 
        dplyr::filter(scoring == "raw") %>%
        dplyr::filter(Contrast == "d.high.vs.low.TypeFreq") %>% 
        dplyr::filter(question == "first.party.acceptablity"))

dat.aov.social.type.token.sequential.acceptability.self.d.high.vs.low.tf <- report.aov(aov.social.type.token.sequential.acceptability.self.d.high.vs.low.tf,
           .correct.for.repeated.tests = FALSE,
           .return.df = TRUE,
           .print.results = FALSE) %>%
    dplyr::select(-c("model.name"))

```


```{r city-acceptability-self-d-high-vs-low-tf-aov-ez-calculate}
aov.social.type.token.sequential.acceptability.self.d.high.vs.low.tf.ez <- ezANOVA(
    data = dat.social.type.token.sequential.m.d %>% 
        dplyr::filter(scoring == "raw") %>% 
        dplyr::filter(Contrast == "d.high.vs.low.TypeFreq") %>% 
        dplyr::filter(question == "first.party.acceptablity"),
    dv = .(d),
    wid = .(filename),
    between = .(myValence),
    within = NULL,
    detailed = TRUE,
    type = 3)

aov.social.type.token.sequential.acceptability.self.d.high.vs.low.tf.ez.sphericity <- 
    extract.sphericity.from.ezANOVA(aov.social.type.token.sequential.acceptability.self.d.high.vs.low.tf.ez)

```
As shown in Table \ref{tab:city-acceptability-self-d-high-vs-low-tf-aov-show}, we observed the expected main effect of Valency on difference scores for first-party acceptability. 


```{r city-acceptability-self-d-high-vs-low-tf-aov-follow-up-valency}

v.valencies <- levels(dat.social.type.token.sequential.m.d$myValence)

dat.aov.social.type.token.sequential.acceptability.self.d.high.vs.low.tf.followup.valency <- 
    purrr::map(v.valencies,
               ~ dat.social.type.token.sequential.m.d %>% 
                   dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
                   dplyr::filter(scoring == "raw") %>% 
                   dplyr::filter(Contrast == "d.high.vs.low.TypeFreq") %>% 
                   dplyr::filter(question == "first.party.acceptablity") %>% 
                   dplyr::mutate(myValence.excluded = .x, .before = 1) %>% 
                   dplyr::filter(myValence != myValence.excluded)) %>% 
    dplyr::bind_rows() %>% 
    dplyr::group_by(myValence.excluded) %>% 
    tidyr::nest() %>% 
    # data is the column created by nest
    dplyr::mutate(aov =  map(data,
                             ~ aov(
                                 d ~ myValence,
                                 data = .x) %>% 
                                 report.aov(.correct.for.repeated.tests = FALSE,
                                            .return.df = TRUE,
                                            .print.results = FALSE)
    )) %>% 
    dplyr::select(-data) %>% 
    unnest(cols = c(aov)) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(myValence =  str_c(v.valencies[!(v.valencies %in% myValence.excluded)], 
                                      collapse = " vs. "), 
                  .before = 1) %>% 
    dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
    dplyr::select(-model.name, -myValence.excluded) 


```
As shown in Table \ref{tab:city-acceptability-self-d-high-vs-low-tf-aov-show}, separate ANOVAs for each valency revealed a strong main effect of Action Status only for neutral scenarios. The main effect was not significant for good or bad scenarios. Again, this is redundant with the direct difference scores.



```{r city-acceptability-self-d-high-vs-low-tf-aov-show}

if (!is.null (aov.social.type.token.sequential.acceptability.self.d.high.vs.low.tf.ez.sphericity)){
    warning ("There were sphericity corrections available that you ignored, do something about them.")    
}


dplyr::bind_rows(
    # Overall anova
    dat.aov.social.type.token.sequential.acceptability.self.d.high.vs.low.tf %>% 
        dplyr::mutate(model.type = "Overall"),
    
    # By valencyy
    dat.aov.social.type.token.sequential.acceptability.self.d.high.vs.low.tf.followup.valency %>% 
        dplyr::mutate(model.type = "By valency")
) %>% 
    dplyr::select(model.type, myValence, effect, everything()) %>% 
        setNames(replace_default_column_labels(names (.))) %>%
        kable.packed("model.type",
                     caption = "ANOVA for difference scores comparing the high vs low type frequency conditions for first-person acceptability. The data combines male and female participants. (Top) Overall ANOVA. (Middle) Differences between frequency conditions separately for each valency for first person acceptability for all participants. (Bottom). Differences for each frequency conditions separately for first person acceptability for all participants.",
                     booktabs = TRUE, escape = FALSE) %>%
    kable_styling(latex_options =
                      c("hold_position", "striped")) %>%
    kableExtra::kable_classic_2()


```



`r clearpage()`

# Correlations

```{r correlations-vs-single-calculate}


l.dat.social.type.token.sequential.corr.vs.single <- dat.social.type.token.sequential.m.wide %>% 
    dplyr::group_by (myValence, Machiavellianism, Narcissism, Psychopathy) %>% 
    dplyr::select(starts_with(fixed ("d.vs.single_"))) %>% 
    dplyr::select(-ends_with(fixed ("_bin"))) %>%
    dplyr::rename_with(~ .x %>% 
                           str_remove(fixed ("d.vs.single_")) %>% 
                           str_remove (fixed ("_raw")) %>% 
                           str_replace_all ("_", " - ") %>% 
                           str_replace_all (fixed ("."), " ") %>% 
                           str_wrap(width = 15)) %>% 
    dplyr::group_by(myValence) %>% 
    dplyr::group_split () %>% 
    map (~ list (myValence = levels2 (.$myValence),
                 corrmat = .x %>% 
                     ungroup %>% 
                     dplyr::select(-c(myValence)) %>% 
                     as.matrix %>% 
                     Hmisc::rcorr (.)))



```

```{r extract-correlations-vs-single}
v.predictors <- c("Machiavellianism", "Narcissism", "Psychopathy")

l.dat.social.type.token.sequential.corr.vs.single %>% 
    map (~ .x$corrmat$P %>% 
             as.data.frame() %>% 
             #dplyr::mutate(across (everything(), ~ .x <= .05)) %>% 
             tibble::rownames_to_column ("var") %>% 
             dplyr::select(-v.predictors) %>% 
             dplyr::rowwise() %>% 
             dplyr::mutate (keep.row = any (dplyr::c_across(-c("var")) <= .05, na.rm= TRUE)) %>% 
             dplyr::filter (keep.row) %>% 
             dplyr::select (-keep.row) %>% 
             dplyr::filter (var %in% v.predictors) %>% 
             tibble::column_to_rownames("var") %>% 
             tdf %>% 
             tibble::rownames_to_column("var") %>% 
             dplyr::rowwise() %>% 
             dplyr::mutate (keep.row = any (dplyr::c_across(-c("var")) <= .05, na.rm= TRUE)) %>% 
             dplyr::filter (keep.row) %>% 
             dplyr::select (-keep.row) %>% 
             dplyr::mutate (myValence = .x$myValence, .before = 1)) %>% 
    dplyr::bind_rows() %>% 
    dplyr::mutate (var = str_replace_all (var, fixed ("\n"), " ")) %>%
    kable.packed (
        "myValence",
        caption = "P values of significant correlations between dark triad predictors and difference scores comparing ratings to the equivalent ratings in the single occurrence condition,",
        escape = TRUE,
       booktabs = TRUE) %>%
kableExtra::kable_classic()
    

```

```{r correlations-high-vs-low-calculate}


l.dat.social.type.token.sequential.corr.high.vs.low <- dat.social.type.token.sequential.m.wide %>% 
    dplyr::group_by (myValence, Machiavellianism, Narcissism, Psychopathy) %>% 
    dplyr::select(starts_with(fixed ("d.high.vs.low.TypeFreq_"))) %>% 
    dplyr::select(-ends_with(fixed ("_bin"))) %>%
    dplyr::rename_with(~ .x %>% 
                           str_remove(fixed ("d.high.vs.low.TypeFreq_")) %>% 
                           str_remove (fixed ("_raw")) %>% 
                           str_replace_all ("_", " - ") %>% 
                           str_replace_all (fixed ("."), " ") %>% 
                           str_wrap(width = 15)) %>% 
    dplyr::group_by(myValence) %>% 
    dplyr::group_split () %>% 
    map (~ list (myValence = levels2 (.$myValence),
                 corrmat = .x %>% 
                     ungroup %>% 
                     dplyr::select(-c(myValence)) %>% 
                     as.matrix %>% 
                     Hmisc::rcorr (.)))



```

```{r extract-correlations-high-vs-low}

l.dat.social.type.token.sequential.corr.high.vs.low %>% 
    map (~ .x$corrmat$P %>% 
             as.data.frame() %>% 
             #dplyr::mutate(across (everything(), ~ .x <= .05)) %>% 
             tibble::rownames_to_column ("var") %>% 
             dplyr::select(-v.predictors) %>% 
             dplyr::rowwise() %>% 
             dplyr::mutate (keep.row = any (dplyr::c_across(-c("var")) <= .05, na.rm= TRUE)) %>% 
             dplyr::filter (keep.row) %>% 
             dplyr::select (-keep.row) %>% 
             dplyr::filter (var %in% v.predictors) %>% 
             tibble::column_to_rownames("var") %>% 
             tdf %>% 
             tibble::rownames_to_column("var") %>% 
             dplyr::rowwise() %>% 
             dplyr::mutate (keep.row = any (dplyr::c_across(-c("var")) <= .05, na.rm= TRUE)) %>% 
             dplyr::filter (keep.row) %>% 
             dplyr::select (-keep.row) %>% 
             dplyr::mutate (myValence = .x$myValence, .before = 1)) %>% 
    dplyr::bind_rows() %>% 
    dplyr::mutate (var = str_replace_all (var, fixed ("\n"), " ")) %>%
    kable.packed (
        "myValence",
        caption = "P values of significant correlations between dark triad predictors and difference scores comparing comparing the high and the low type frequency conditions.",
        escape = TRUE,
       booktabs = TRUE) %>%
kableExtra::kable_classic()


```

```{r correlations-vs-single-plot, fig.cap = "Correlation for difference scores comparing actions against single occurrence actions.", fig.height=17}


par(mfrow=c(3, 1),  
    mar = c(1, 1, 1, 1))
    #xpd = TRUE)

l.dat.social.type.token.sequential.corr.vs.single %>% 
    purrr::walk(~ corrplot::corrplot(.x$corrmat$r,
                       is.corr = TRUE,
                       type = "lower",
                       diag = FALSE,
                       tl.col = "blue",
                       tl.cex = 1.5,
                       cl.cex = .5,
                       #bg = "gray",
                       tl.srt = 75,
                       title = str_c ("\n\n\n\n\n", .x$myValence),
                       addCoef.col = "black",
                       addgrid.col = "black",
                       #order = "hclust",
                       p.mat = .x$corrmat$P,
                       insig = "label_sig",
                       sig.level = .05,
                       mar = c(1, 1, 1, 1)
                       ))






```

```{r correlations-high-vs-low-plot, fig.cap = "Correlation for difference scores comparing high vs. low type frequency actions.", fig.height=17}


par(mfrow=c(3, 1),  
    mar = c(1, 1, 1, 1))
    #xpd = TRUE)

l.dat.social.type.token.sequential.corr.high.vs.low %>% 
    purrr::walk(~ corrplot::corrplot(.x$corrmat$r,
                       is.corr = TRUE,
                       type = "lower",
                       diag = FALSE,
                       tl.col = "blue",
                       tl.cex = 1.5,
                       cl.cex = .5,
                       #bg = "gray",
                       tl.srt = 75,
                       title = str_c ("\n\n\n\n\n", .x$myValence),
                       addCoef.col = "black",
                       addgrid.col = "black",
                       #order = "hclust",
                       p.mat = .x$corrmat$P,
                       insig = "label_sig",
                       sig.level = .05,
                       mar = c(1, 1, 1, 1)
                       ))






```

`r clearpage()`

# Binary ratings (analyzed using GLMER)
## Ratings

```{r print-descriptives-ratings-bin}

dat.social.type.token.sequential.m %>% 
    dplyr::filter(scoring == "bin") %>% 
    dplyr::select(-scoring) %>% 
    dplyr::group_by(myValence, myQueriedActionStatus, question) %>% 
    dplyr::summarize(
        N = n(),
        M = mean(rating),
        SE = se(rating)
        ) %>% 
    dplyr::arrange (question, myValence, myQueriedActionStatus) %>% 
    tidyr::unite(pack.col, myValence, question, sep = " - ") %>% 
    kable.packed("pack.col", 
                 caption = "Descriptives for the data into binary ratings.",
                 col.names = c("", "N", "M", "SE"),
                 booktabs = TRUE,
                 longtable=TRUE) %>% 
    kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = "repeat_header") %>% 
    kableExtra::kable_classic_2()


```

```{r plot-acurracy-bin, fig.cap = "Bin ratings"}

dat.social.type.token.sequential.m %>% 
    dplyr::filter (scoring == "bin") %>% 
    dplyr::mutate (myQueriedActionStatus = str_wrap(myQueriedActionStatus, 10)) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate (question = str_replace_all(question, fixed ("."), " ")) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate (question = str_wrap(question, 20)) %>% 
    ggplot (aes (x = myQueriedActionStatus, y = rating)) +
        theme_light(14) +
    theme (axis.title.x = element_blank(),
           axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, vjust = .5, hjust=.5)) + 
    ylab ("Rating (binary)") + 
    geom_violin() +
    geom_dotplot(binaxis='y', stackdir='center', dotsize=1) +
    stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se, 
                 geom="pointrange", color="red") +
    geom_hline(yintercept=.5, linetype = "dotted") +
    #scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Pre-segmented", "Continuous (1)", "Continuous (2)"))
    facet_grid(question ~ myValence)



```

### Prevalence 



```{r city-prevalence-glmer-bin-calculate}


dat.social.type.token.sequential.for.glmm <- dat.social.type.token.sequential %>% 
        dplyr::filter(scoring == "bin") %>% 
        dplyr::mutate(myQueriedActionStatus = factor(myQueriedActionStatus,
                                                     levels = c("single", "lowTypeFreq", "highTypeFreq")),
                      myValence = factor(myValence, 
                                         levels = c("neutral", "bad", "good"))) %>% 
        dplyr::mutate(across(c(filename, myQueriedAction), factor))


lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.prevalence.1 <- glmer(
    rating ~ myQueriedActionStatus * myValence + 
        (1|filename) + (1|myQueriedAction), 
    control=glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa"),
    family="binomial",
    data = dat.social.type.token.sequential.for.glmm %>% 
                dplyr::filter(question == "prevalence"))

    
# Remove random factor  
lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.prevalence.2 <- update(
    lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.prevalence.1,
    ~ . - (1|myQueriedAction))  
    
# anova(lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.prevalence.1,
#       lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.prevalence.2)

## Remove interaction
lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.prevalence.3 <- update(
    lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.prevalence.1,
    ~ . - myQueriedActionStatus:myValence)  

# anova(lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.prevalence.1,
#       lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.prevalence.3)

# Keep full model

lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.prevalence.results <- 
    extract.results.from.binary.model(lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.prevalence.1)


```


### Third-party acceptability

```{r city-acceptability-other-glmer-bin-calculate}


lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.other.1 <- glmer(
    rating ~ myQueriedActionStatus * myValence + 
        (1|filename) + (1|myQueriedAction), 
    control = glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa"),
    family = "binomial",
    data = dat.social.type.token.sequential.for.glmm %>% 
        dplyr::filter(question == "third.party.acceptability"))

    
# Remove random factor  
lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.other.2 <- update(
    lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.other.1,
    ~ . - (1|myQueriedAction))  
    
# anova(lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.other.1,
#       lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.other.2)

## Remove interaction
lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.other.3 <- update(
    lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.other.1,
    ~ . - myQueriedActionStatus:myValence)  

# anova(lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.other.1,
#       lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.other.3)

# Keep full model

lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.other.results <- 
    extract.results.from.binary.model(lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.other.1)



    
```



### First-party acceptability

```{r city-acceptability-self-glmer-bin-calculate}


lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.self.1 <- glmer(
    rating ~ myQueriedActionStatus * myValence + 
        (1|filename) + (1|myQueriedAction), 
    control = glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa"),
    family = "binomial",
    data = dat.social.type.token.sequential.for.glmm %>% 
        dplyr::filter(question == "first.party.acceptablity"))

    
# Remove random factor  
lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.self.2 <- update(
    lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.self.1,
    ~ . - (1|myQueriedAction))  
    
# anova(lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.self.1,
#       lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.self.2)

## Remove interaction
lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.self.3 <- update(
    lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.self.1,
    ~ . - myQueriedActionStatus:myValence)  

# anova(lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.self.1,
#       lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.self.3)

# Keep full model for consistency

lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.self.results <- 
    extract.results.from.binary.model(lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.self.1)
```


```{r city-glmer-print}
dplyr::bind_rows(
    lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.prevalence.results %>% 
        dplyr::mutate(pack.col = "Prevalence", .before = 1),
    
lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.other.results %>% 
        dplyr::mutate(pack.col = "Third party acceptability", .before = 1),

lmer.social.type.token.sequential.city.bin.acceptability.self.results %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(pack.col = "First party acceptability", .before = 1)
) %>% 
    dplyr::select (-c("t_log", "p_log")) %>% 
    #filter (!grepl ("Intercept", term)) %>% 
    kable.packed (
    "pack.col",
    caption = "GLMER results, using the full models.",
    col.names = str_remove(names (.[,-1]), "_.*$"),
       booktabs = TRUE, escape = FALSE) %>%
    kableExtra::kable_classic() %>%
    kableExtra::add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "Log-odds" = 3, "Odd ratios" = 3, " " = 2),
                                 bold = TRUE) %>% 
    kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options =
                                  c("scale_down",
                              "hold_position",
                              "full_width"))

```







## Difference scores (GLMER or ANOVA?)

```{r plot-difference-vs-single-bin, fig.cap = "Binary difference scores vs. single occurrence actions"}

dat.social.type.token.sequential.m.wide %>% 
    group_by (myValence) %>% 
    dplyr::select(starts_with(fixed ("d."))) %>% 
    dplyr::select(ends_with(fixed ("_bin"))) %>% 
    dplyr::select(contains (fixed ("vs.single"))) %>% 
    tidyr::pivot_longer(-c(myValence),
                        names_to = "Contrast",
                        values_to = "d") %>% 
    dplyr::mutate (Contrast = Contrast %>% 
                       str_remove ("_bin$") %>% 
                       str_remove ("^d\\.vs\\.single\\_")) %>% 
    tidyr::separate(Contrast, c("myQueriedActionStatus", "question"), sep = "_") %>% 
    dplyr::mutate (question = str_replace_all(question, fixed ("."), " ")) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate (question = str_wrap(question, 20)) %>% 
    ggplot (aes (x = myQueriedActionStatus, y = d)) +
        theme_light(14) +
    theme (axis.title.x = element_blank(),
           axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, vjust = .5, hjust=.5)) + 
    ylab ("Difference score (binary)") + 
    geom_violin() +
    geom_dotplot(binaxis='y', stackdir='center', dotsize=1) +
    stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se, 
                 geom="pointrange", color="red") +
    geom_hline(yintercept=0, linetype = "dotted") +
    #scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Pre-segmented", "Continuous (1)", "Continuous (2)"))
    facet_grid(question ~ myValence)




```


```{r plot-difference-high-vs-low-type-frequency-bin, fig.cap = "Binary difference scores high vs. low type frequency actions"}

dat.social.type.token.sequential.m.wide %>% 
    group_by (myValence) %>% 
    dplyr::select(starts_with(fixed ("d."))) %>% 
    dplyr::select(ends_with(fixed ("_bin"))) %>% 
    dplyr::select(-contains (fixed ("vs.single"))) %>% 
    tidyr::pivot_longer(-c(myValence),
                        names_to = "question",
                        values_to = "d") %>%
    dplyr::mutate (question = question %>% 
                       str_remove ("_bin$") %>% 
                       str_remove ("^d\\.high\\.vs\\.low\\.TypeFreq_")) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate (question = str_replace_all(question, fixed ("."), " ")) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate (question = str_wrap(question, 20)) %>% 
    ggplot (aes (x = question, y = d)) +
        theme_light(14) +
    theme (axis.title.x = element_blank(),
           axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, vjust = .5, hjust=.5)) + 
    ylab ("Difference score (binary)") + 
    geom_violin() +
    geom_dotplot(binaxis='y', stackdir='center', dotsize=1) +
    stat_summary(fun.data=mean_se, 
                 geom="pointrange", color="red") +
    geom_hline(yintercept=0, linetype = "dotted") +
    #scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Pre-segmented", "Continuous (1)", "Continuous (2)"))
    facet_grid(. ~ myValence)




```

### Prevalence 

### Third-party acceptability

### First-party acceptability
